mappings.rationale: Directly covers group structure of society including families, minorities, social problems, and welfare services.
Covers group dynamics, prejudice, intergroup relations, and the psychological dimensions of group membership.
Directly covers the social effects of racial and ethnic prejudice (subsection B).
Covers minorities and ethnic groups and their integration into wider society (subsection A.4).
Covers a specific form of ethnic prejudice and its social effects (subsection B).
Directly addresses women's social and legal status as a special social concern (subsection C.3).
Covers philosophical dimensions of women's social status and gender-based group dynamics.
Covers the historical development of women's social and legal status (subsection C.3).
Covers a pioneer of arguments for women's social and legal rights, relevant to subsection C.3.
Directly covers adolescence as a special social concern (subsection C.1).
Directly covers old age as a special social concern (subsection C.2).
Relevant to child welfare and the psychological aspects of youth social concerns.
Covers the historical treatment of children as a social group, relevant to child and youth welfare (subsections D.2.b and D.2.c).
Directly covers poverty as a special social concern (subsection C.5).
Directly covers criminality and delinquency as special social concerns (subsection C.6).
Covers organized criminality as a social concern (subsection C.6).
Directly covers sexual orientation as a special social concern (subsection C.7).
Covers drug use and pharmacology relevant to drug and alcohol abuse as a social concern (subsection C.9).
Directly covers drug and alcohol abuse and addiction as special social concerns (subsection C.9).
Covers medical and psychiatric social services and mental health as a social concern (subsection D.2.g).
Covers psychological services relevant to medical and psychiatric social services (subsection D.2.g).
Covers depression as a mental health concern relevant to psychiatric social services.
Covers psychopathic behaviour relevant to criminality and psychiatric social concerns.
Covers schizophrenia as relevant to psychiatric social services (subsection D.2.g).
Directly covers organized public activities to alleviate social needs, including welfare services (subsection D).
Directly covers social service as organized activities to address human wants and needs (subsection D).
Covers the modern family's legal organization and functions (subsection A.2).
Covers public health as a field of social service relevant to community welfare and medical social services.
Covers diaspora communities as minorities and ethnic groups within broader societies (subsection A.4).
Covers immigrant groups as minorities within societies and related social concerns.
Covers refugees as a special social group requiring welfare services and community support.
Covers segregated communities as a form of minority group experience and social concern.
Covers violence as a social phenomenon related to criminality and delinquency (subsection C.6).
Covers dementia as a condition relevant to old age social concerns and medical social services (subsections C.2 and D.2.g).
Covers autism as a condition relevant to special educational and social service needs.
Covers the struggle against racial prejudice and for equal social status of minority groups.
Covers the experience of a major ethnic minority group and the social effects of racial prejudice.
Covers surveillance as a mechanism of social control relevant to group monitoring and privacy concerns.
Covers the rights framework protecting minorities, women, and vulnerable groups from discrimination and abuse.
Covers public health crises requiring disaster relief and community welfare responses (subsection D.2.e).
Covers HIV/AIDS as a social concern requiring medical and community social services.
Covers historical and social dimensions of mental illness as a social concern requiring psychiatric social services.
